questions.clue: This Indian religion teaches a path to spiritual purity and enlightenment through disciplined nonviolence toward all living creatures.

questions.clue: This Islamic head covering wraps around the head to cover the hair, neck and ears while leaving the face exposed.

citations.title: Hijab
citations.snippet: The Hijab, while commonly equated with the headscarf worn by Muslim women, is a term also associated with a broader range of bodily covering and behavioral patterns that extend to human emotions. When used in the sense of headscarf, this te

questions.clue: The herald of the Olympians who wore winged sandals, this deity was patron of travelers and thieves and guided the souls of the dead to the underworld.

citations.title: Hermes
citations.snippet: Hermes is an Olympian deity in ancient Greek religion and mythology considered the herald of the gods. He is also widely considered the protector of human heralds, travelers, thieves, merchants, and orators. He is able to move quickly and f

questions.clue: A short Hebrew scripture that mourns the fall of Jerusalem in 586 BCE, this book appears in the Ketuvim as one of the Five Megillot alongside Ruth, Esther, and Ecclesiastes.

citations.title: Book of Lamentations
citations.snippet: The Book of Lamentations is a collection of poetic laments for the destruction of Jerusalem in 586 BCE. In the Hebrew Bible, it appears in the Ketuvim ("Writings") as one of the Five Megillot alongside the Song of Songs, Book of Ruth, Eccle

questions.clue: Also called the Catholic Revival, this period of institutional Church resurgence was initiated as a comprehensive response to the Protestant movement that challenged papal authority in the 16th century.

citations.title: Counter-Reformation

questions.clue: Also known as Progressive or Liberal, this denomination holds that its faith's laws must evolve with each generation, placing moral duties above ceremonial ones.

citations.title: Reform Judaism

questions.clue: This winged horse of Greek myth sprang from the blood of the Gorgon when Perseus slew her, its father Poseidon having taken on his equine aspect.

citations.title: Pegasus
citations.snippet: Pegasus is a winged horse in Greek mythology, usually depicted as a white stallion. He was sired by Poseidon, in his role as horse-god, and foaled by the Gorgon Medusa. Pegasus was the brother of Chrysaor, both born from Medusa's blood when

questions.clue: This legendary Thracian musician sailed with Jason and the Argonauts before making a separate journey into the realm of the dead to reclaim his lost wife.

citations.title: Orpheus
citations.snippet: In Greek mythology, Orpheus was a Thracian bard, legendary musician and prophet. He was also a renowned poet and, according to legend, travelled with Jason and the Argonauts in search of the Golden Fleece, and descended into the underworld

questions.clue: Born Jorge Mario Bergoglio in Buenos Aires, this pontiff became in 2013 the first member of the Society of Jesus to serve as head of the Catholic Church.

citations.title: Pope Francis
citations.snippet: Pope Francis was the head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of Vatican City from 13 March 2013 until his death in 2025. He was the first Jesuit pope, the first Latin American, and the first pope born or raised outside Europe since the 8t

questions.clue: In this form of philosophical dialogue, a questioner probes a partner with systematic questions and clarifications until the partner either reaches a conclusion independently or must admit the limits of their knowledge.

citations.title: Socratic method

questions.clue: This cognitive process elicits both emotional and rational associations from an individual's moral framework, producing the inner sense of guilt or approval that guides ethical decisions.

citations.title: Conscience
citations.snippet: A conscience is a cognitive process that elicits emotion and rational associations based on an individual's moral philosophy or value system. Conscience is not an elicited emotion or thought produced by associations based on immediate senso

questions.clue: Derived from a Philistine deity once worshipped in Ekron, this demon's name in Abrahamic tradition translates as 'Lord of the Flies' and is associated with the Canaanite god Baal.

citations.title: Beelzebub

questions.clue: A Roman Christian adolescent executed in 304 CE for refusing to deny her faith, this virgin martyr is venerated as a patron saint and commemorated by the Catholic Church on January 21.

citations.title: Agnes of Rome

questions.clue: Born in Syria, this 8th-century pontiff was the last non-European head of the Catholic Church until a Latin American was elected to lead it in 2013.

citations.title: Pope Francis
